preview_text:
The model favors England decisively, assigning them an 87% win probability. Ghana's chance registers at 0%, while a draw holds 13%. This represents a substantial gap between the two sides in the model's estimation.

The W/D/L distribution reflects a wide separation in expected quality. England's win probability dominates the outcome space, with the draw as the only realistic alternative to an England victory. The model treats a Ghana upset as effectively impossible within its framework, suggesting little competitive balance on the underlying ratings.

The sportsbook consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Books price England at 71%, some 16 percentage points lower, and assign Ghana 11% — a material difference. The market also rates a draw more highly at 19% versus the model's 13%. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is more accurate; the divergence likely reflects different methodologies, information sets, or risk adjustment across the markets.
